XOnlineChangeLogonUsers

Creates an asynchronous task to change the set of local players logged onto Xbox Live.

Note    Titles using XHV must free the XHV engine (by calling Release on the IXHVEngine pointer) before calling XOnlineChangeLogonUsers. After the logon player change is complete, the title can re-initialize the XHV engine if desired.

HRESULT XOnlineChangeLogonUsers(
    const XONLINE_USER *pUsers,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE pHandle
);

Parameters

pUsers
[in] Array of XONLINE_USER structures that contain information about the players using the console who would like to participate in the online game session. The array must contain XONLINE_MAX_LOGON_USERS structures.

The order of users represented in the array is significant and must map one-to-one to the controller index (zero-based) of the game port that the player is using. If no player is using a specific game port, the XONLINE_USER structure at that index should be set to all zeroes.

Use the XOnlineGetUsers function to obtain XONLINE_USER structures that contain player information stored on the local Xbox hard disk or in Memory Units.

hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle to an event that will be signaled when there is work to be done on the logon change task. The event will be left unsignaled if the task is currently blocked by an asynchronous operation. This parameter can be NULL if no such event is required.
pHandle
[out] Pointer to a variable of type XONLINETASK_HANDLE that receives a task handle representing this logon change task. The handle can then be used in calls to XOnlineTaskContinue to perform work associated with this task.

Return Values

The return value indicates whether the asynchronous task was successfully created:

Value Description
S_OK The asynchronous logon change task was successfully created.
XONLINE_E_NO_GUEST_ACCESS A player logged on as a guest does not have a host present.
XONLINE_E_OUT_OF_MEMORY There was not enough memory to create the asynchronous logon change task.
XONLINE_E_TASK_BUSY Another logon task is currently being performed, or the previous logon task has not been closed. (In debug builds, an assert will occur when this value is returned.)

The return value does not represent the success or failure of the actual logon change task. Once the logon change task has been successfully created, XOnlineTaskContinue should be called until it returns a value other than XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING. At that point, the return value determines how to proceed (see the Remarks section for details). The possible return values from XOnlineTaskContinue for the logon change task are:

Value Description
XONLINE_S_LOGON_USER_CHANGE_COMPLETE The logon change has been successfully completed. The task handle should be closed.
XONLINE_S_LOGON_COMMIT_USER_CHANGE The first phase of the logon change task has completed. The logon change task can no longer be aborted without also disconnecting the existing logon. Many online functions may not be called while the logon is in this state, especially those that rely on the indices of logged on players. XOnlineTaskContinue should continue to be called until it indicates final completion or an error.
XONLINE_E_LOGON_CHANGE_USER_FAILED An error concerning one of the new or existing players has occurred that prevents the change from proceeding. XOnlineChangeLogonUsersTaskGetResults can be called to retrieve error results for each of the users specified in the logon change request. If this error occurs before the change task has reached the XONLINE_S_LOGON_COMMIT_USER_CHANGE phase, then existing players can continue to play. Otherwise, the existing logon session must be terminated.
XONLINE_E_LOGON_CANNOT_ACCESS_SERVICE One or more problems were encountered trying to log onto the online service. If this error occurs before the change task has reached the XONLINE_S_LOGON_COMMIT_USER_CHANGE phase, then existing users can continue to play. Otherwise, the existing logon session must be terminated and the player should be given the option to reboot into the network troubleshooting portion of the Xbox Dashboard.
XONLINE_E_LOGON_SERVERS_TOO_BUSY The logon servers are currently too busy to perform the logon request. The existing logon session must be terminated, and the player should be instructed to try to log on again later.
XONLINE_E_LOGON_UPDATE_REQUIRED The title should terminate the existing logon session and call XOnlineTitleUpdate to update the title.

Remarks

XOnlineChangeLogonUsers returns an asynchronous task that must be pumped with XOnlineTaskContinue in order to perform work on and eventually complete the task. The task normally proceeds through two different phases before finally completing.

XOnlineTaskContinue should first be called for the logon change task until it returns a value other than XONLINETASK_S_RUNNING, indicating that either the first phase of the change (or possibly the entire task) has completed, or that an error has occurred. A return value of XONLINE_S_LOGON_COMMIT_USER_CHANGE indicates that the first phase has been completed, and that the change request task is now committed to proceeding with the logon change. At this point, the logon change task can no longer be aborted without also disconnecting the existing logon. Many online functions may not be called while the logon change is in this state, especially those that rely on the indices of logged on players (typically, those that take a user index as an argument). The first phase has completed and XOnlineTaskContinue should continue to be called until it indicates final completion or an error.

If an error value is returned before the first phase is completed, the logon change has failed, but, depending on the specific error, the existing logged on users may be able to continue playing. XONLINE_E_LOGON_CHANGE_USER_FAILED and XONLINE_E_LOGON_CANNOT_ACCESS_SERVICE allow the game to continue with the existing players, while XONLINE_E_LOGON_SERVERS_TOO_BUSY and XONLINE_E_LOGON_UPDATE_REQUIRED require terminating the existing logon session.

After the first phase has been completed, XOnlineTaskContinue should continue to be called until it returns an error code or returns XONLINE_S_LOGON_USER_CHANGE_COMPLETE to indicate that the second phase has succeeded and that the change is complete. Once the change has succeeded, normal calling of online functions may resume, with the newly logged on players participating. The user indices specified for users now refer to the index in the new array returned by XOnlineGetLogonUsers. The logon change task handle should be closed. Games with game servers that have requested "authdata" will need to notify the game server of the player change and have the game server request the data again. Game hosts that have collected user identifiers and gamertags of participating players will need to be informed of the new players.

If the logon change task returns an error value after the first phase has been completed, the change task has failed, the task handle should be closed, and the existing logon session terminated as well.

In some cases, the first phase of the task may be not be needed, and XOnlineTaskContinue will return XONLINE_S_LOGON_USER_CHANGE_COMPLETE prior to (and instead of) returning XONLINE_S_LOGON_COMMIT_USER_CHANGE. This indicates that the first phase was not necessary, and that the change has completed successfully.

Note that, in order for the logon change task to eventually succeed, both the task handle returned in pHandle as well as the original logon task handle returned by XOnlineLogon must be pumped with XOnlineTaskContinue.

The game's user interface should give players on each individual controller the option of logging off or of directly switching to a different player for that controller. In the case where a different player is logging on at a particular controller in place of a previous player, call XOnlineChangeLogonUsers only once, with the new player assigned to that controller, not twice (once to log off the old player and once to log on the new player).

XOnlineChangeLogonUsers cannot be called to change from having one or more players to having no players, or to change from having no players to having one or more players.

After a successful logon change, XOnlineGetLogonUsers will return information for the new list of players.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

Xbox Live Functions | XOnlineLogon, XOnlineTaskContinue, XOnlineChangeLogonUsersTaskGetResults
